description: Chandler opens a purple apartment door and walks inside.
Chandler says, "Hey."
Ross, Joey, Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe are seated around a coffee table playing cards. Monica turns her head toward Chandler as other voices overlap with the greeting.
Monica asks, "So how is Joan?"
Chandler steps closer and says, "Broke up with her." The group vocalizes surprise. Ross asks, "Oh, why? Don't tell me. Because of the big nostril thing?"
Chandler looks down at Ross.
Chandler gestures with his right hand and says, "They were huge."
Chandler sits on the arm of a chair.
Chandler continues, "When she sneezed, bats flew out of them."
Rachel, resting her chin on her hand, says, "Come on, they were not that huge."
Chandler replies, "I'm telling you, she leaned back, I could see her brain," gesturing toward his own head.
Monica raises her hands from her lap.
Monica says, "How many perfectly fine women are you going to reject over the most superficial, insignificant--"
Joey raises his hands and interrupts, saying, "hold it."
Joey points toward Chandler and says, "I mean, I got a side with Chandler on this one."
Joey gestures with his hands and continues, "When I first moved to the city I went out a couple times with this girl. Really hot,"
Joey adds, "great kisser. But she had the biggest Adams apple," placing his hand near his own throat.
Joey lowers his eyebrows, tightens his lips, and shakes his hands slightly.
Joey finishes, "nuts," dropping his hands to his lap.
Chandler and Ross exchange a glance.
Chandler points at Ross, asking, "You or me?" Ross replies, "I got it."
Ross turns his head to face Joey.
Ross says, "Joey, women don't have Adam's apples."
Joey's gaze remains fixed on Ross, his jaw slightly open. Audience laughter becomes audible.
Joey shifts his gaze back and forth between Ross and Chandler, maintaining the same expression while the audience laughter continues.
Joey points a finger at them, smiles, and says, "You guys are messing with me, right?"
Joey laughs, slaps Ross on the shoulder, and rubs his own knee as audience laughter swells.
Joey says, "That was a good one. For a second there, I was like, whoa," gesturing with his hand.
The setting changes to a kitchen. Phoebe places two beer bottles on a small round table where Chandler and Ross are seated. Monica stands near the refrigerator in the background.
Phoebe sits down at the table. Joey stands near the counter while Monica steps forward.
Phoebe looks at Chandler and says, "So you name one woman that you broke up with for an actual, real reason?"
Chandler gestures with the bottle in his hand.
Chandler says, "Maureen Rosillo." Ross interjects, "Because she doesn't hate Yanni. Is not a real"
A knocking sound occurs. Monica walks to the purple front door and pulls it open, revealing an older man in a robe standing in the hallway.
The older man says, "hello?" Monica replies, "Hello? Mr."
Monica says, "Heckles," standing in the doorway.
Mr. Heckles stands in the hallway.
Mr. Heckles says, "you're doing it again." Monica replies, "We're not doing anything."
Mr. Heckles says, "You're stomping. It's disturbing my birds." Rachel steps up behind Monica.
Monica looks back at Rachel.
Rachel says, "You don't have birds." Mr. Heckles replies, "I could have birds."
Monica tilts her head and looks at him.
Monica says, "Okay, Mr. Heckles. We'll try to keep it down."
Mr. Heckles says, "Thank you."
Mr. Heckles says, "I'm going to rejoin my dinner party."
Mr. Heckles turns around and walks toward his own door.
Monica says, "All right. Bye bye."
Monica closes the door, and she and Rachel walk back into the apartment.
Monica walks past the kitchen table. Chandler says, "Okay, Janice. Janice. You got to give me Janice. That wasn't about being picky."
Ross says, "Yeah. We'll give you Janice." Phoebe adds, "I miss Janice, though."
Phoebe looks off to the side, smiling slightly.
Phoebe adopts a nasal voice quality and says, "Hello, Chandler Bing."
Rachel throws both hands up in the air and laughs.
Rachel speaks with a similar nasal voice quality and says, "Oh, my God."
Joey walks toward the counter, holding a long loaf of bread.
Joey holds up the bread and says, "Oh, Chandler," maintaining the nasal voice quality.
Joey says, "Now?"
Chandler looks at Joey.
Chandler replies, "Yes," matching the nasal voice quality.
Joey pulls the loaf of bread apart with visible force. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey says, "That's it," pointing with the torn bread.
Joey gestures again with the bread.
Joey says, "There. Faster."
Chandler, Ross, Monica, Phoebe, and Rachel laugh while Joey holds the bread and smiles.
A rhythmic thumping noise comes from beneath the floorboards. The group's laughter stops as they look downward.
Monica stomps her foot on the floor and shouts, "Stop with the broom. We're not making noise."
Monica stomps repeatedly. Joey and Rachel walk over and join her, stomping their feet on the floor.
The three continue to stomp rhythmically. The thumping noise from below ceases.
They stop stomping and stand silently, listening.
Rachel smiles and says, "We won." Another voice adds, "We do."
They smile and high-five. The scene changes to a hallway where a person lies on a stretcher, covered entirely by a pale blue sheet. Medics and a police officer stand nearby.
Monica, standing with the group in the hallway, says, "Mr. Heckles."
The medics wheel the stretcher past the group.
Chandler asks, "how did this happen?"
Mr. Treeger, holding a coffee cup, replies, "He must have been sweeping. They found a broom in his hand."
Ross places his hand on his stomach, and the screen turns black.
In a hallway, Rachel stands with one hand pressed to her mouth, looking toward Mr. Treeger. Monica stands beside her, looking at Mr. Treeger.
Rachel says, "That's terrible." Mr. Treeger replies, "I know." Ross, standing behind Mr. Treeger with his hand on his stomach, says, "I was sweeping yesterday."
Ross slightly shakes his head.
Ross says, "Could have been me."
Phoebe and Chandler stand nearby, looking toward Ross. Mr. Treeger raises a donut to his mouth and takes a bite.
Mr. Treeger says, "You never know," while chewing.
Ross replies, "Never know," his voice soft.

The group stands quietly in the hallway. The setting changes to an apartment living room where Joey, Ross, and Chandler sit around a coffee table, and Monica pours drinks. Phoebe walks past the couch holding a mug.
Phoebe, stopping and holding her free hand out slightly, says, "Very faint, but I can still sense him in the building."
Phoebe raises her free hand higher, her palm facing forward.
Phoebe waves her hand and says, "Go into the light, Mr."
She continues to wave her hand upward.
Phoebe finishes, "Heckles."
Ross looks at Phoebe, then briefly down at the table. Phoebe brings her mug to her lips.
Ross says, "Okay, Phoebe," as she drinks. Phoebe lowers her mug and says, "I'm sorry, but sometimes they need help."
Chandler, holding a mug, and Rachel sit near each other. Monica sits on the couch next to Joey, eating.
Monica gestures with a piece of food and says, "Here we go." Phoebe, lowering herself to sit on the edge of the coffee table, says, "That's fine."
Phoebe says, "Go ahead and know. There are a lot of things out there that I don't believe in but that doesn't mean they're not true."
Joey looks at Phoebe, his mouth slightly open.
Joey asks, "Such as?"
Phoebe replies, "Like crop circles or the Bermuda Triangle, or," gesturing with her hands.
Ross raises his eyebrows and stares at Phoebe. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross, gesturing with his hand, says, "don't. You don't believe in evolution?" Phoebe replies, "No, not really," shaking her head slightly.
Ross stares at Phoebe, leaning forward slightly with his mouth open.
Ross repeats, "You don't believe in evolution?" his volume rising. Phoebe looks away, saying, "I don't know. It's just monkeys."
Phoebe slightly nods her head.
Phoebe continues, "Darwin. It's a nice story. I just think it's a little too easy."
Monica looks at Joey, who sits with his arms crossed. Ross stares at Phoebe, his jaw tense. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross asks, "Too easy?" his volume louder than his previous line.
Ross gestures with his hands, moving them up and down.
Ross continues, "The process of every living thing on this planet," his pace increasing.
Ross finishes, "evolving over millions of years from single celled organisms is too," his hands moving as he speaks.
Ross finishes, "easy?" Phoebe replies, "Yeah, I just don't buy it," brushing her hair back slightly.
Ross leans forward, pointing his finger toward Phoebe.
Ross says, "Excuse me?"
Ross continues, "Evolution is not for you to buy, Phoebe. Evolution is scientific fact," pointing his finger rhythmically.
Ross finishes, "like the air we breathe. Like," maintaining his pointing gesture.
Ross finishes, "gravity," gesturing with his hand open.
Phoebe holds up her hands and says, "Okay, don't get me started on gravity."
Chandler and Rachel look at Phoebe. Ross stares at Phoebe, briefly raising a hand to his ear. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross asks, "You don't believe in gravity?"
Phoebe looks down at her hands, shifting them in her lap.
Phoebe says, "Well, it's not so much that I don't believe in it," gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe gestures outward with both hands.
Phoebe continues, "I don't know, lately I get the feeling," moving her hands downward.
She adds, "that I'm not so much being pulled down as I am being pushed," continuing her downward hand motion.
Ross stares at Phoebe, his mouth closed. Joey and Monica sit quietly on the couch. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler, seated on the armchair, says, "Uh oh. It's Isaac Newton. And he's pissed," holding his mug.
Chandler lowers his mug and looks toward the ceiling.
Chandler says, "Quick, Phoebes. Up on the," motioning upward with his hand.
A knocking sound is audible. The setting changes to the apartment doorway, where Monica opens the door to reveal Mr. Treeger and Buddy Boyle standing in the hallway.
Mr. Treeger steps forward and says, "oh, there she is. And over there, that's the other one," pointing into the apartment.
Mr. Treeger adds, "This is Mr. Buddy Boyle, Heckles' attorney."
Mr. Treeger finishes, "He'd like to talk to you." Monica asks, "What can we do for you?"
Buddy Boyle walks into the apartment, sets his briefcase on the kitchen table, and says, "All right, kids, here's the deal."
Buddy Boyle continues, "According to my client's will, he wants to leave all his earthly possessions," while opening the briefcase.
He continues, "to the," taking out a document.
He looks at the document in his hand.
He reads from the document, "noisy girls in the apartment above mine."
Monica and Rachel look at each other with their mouths open. The rest of the group stands in the background.
Monica asks, "But what about his family?" Buddy Boyle replies, "He didn't have any."
Rachel says, "Whoa," leaning back slightly.
Rachel looks downward, her eyes wide.
Rachel continues, "okay, so let's talk money," her volume slightly louder.
Buddy Boyle looks at Rachel and shakes his head slightly.
Buddy Boyle says, "All right, there was none."
Buddy Boyle gestures with a pen and says, "Now let's talk signing. You be noisy girl number one. You be noisy girl number two," pointing at Monica, then Rachel.
Monica looks at Rachel, who looks back at her.
Monica says, "I can't believe that this whole time we thought he hated us," turning back to Rachel.
Monica continues, "Isn't it amazing how much you can touch someone's life without even knowing it?"
The setting changes to a very messy, cluttered apartment. Chandler, standing amidst piles of junk with Rachel and Monica, says, "Would you look at this dump? He"
Chandler continues, "hated us. This was his final revenge. Ever seen so much crap?" as Rachel picks up a small doll-like object.
Ross, standing next to Chandler and Rachel, says, "Actually, I think this apartment sullies the good name of crap," as Rachel looks at the object in her hand.
Chandler looks toward the hallway and says, "Check this out."
Joey walks into the room holding a large, clear glass dome positioned in front of his groin, the curved glass magnifying the area.
Joey looks at the others and asks, "Can I have this?"
Ross and Phoebe stand near a large pile of newspapers. Ross looks at Phoebe.
Ross gestures with one hand and asks, "How can you not believe in evolution?"
Phoebe turns a patterned, sleeveless shirt over in her hands.
Phoebe says, "Just don't. Look at this funky shirt," holding the shirt up.
Ross raises his hands to his chest and says, "I've studied evolution my entire adult life, okay?"
Ross continues, "And I can tell you we have collected fossils from all over the world that actually show"
Ross gestures outward with his hands and continues, "the evolution of different species, okay?"
Ross says, "I mean, you can literally see them evolving through"
Ross finishes, "time," lowering his hands. Phoebe looks at him, tilting her head slightly.
Phoebe asks, "Really? You can actually see it?" Ross nods and replies, "You bet."
Ross says, "In the US," gesturing with his hand.
Ross continues, "China, Africa, all over."
Phoebe raises her eyebrows.
Phoebe says, "See, I didn't know that." Ross gestures toward her and says, "Well, there you go."
Phoebe crosses her arms, her gaze shifting downward.
Phoebe says, "So now the real question is who put those fossils there and why?"
Ross stares at Phoebe, his mouth slightly open and his posture still.
Chandler walks over holding a large black book and says, "Hey, look at this. My Big Book of Grievances." He opens the book.
Joey, Ross, and Monica step closer and gather around Chandler to look at the open pages.
Joey points at the page and says, "Hey, there's me."
Joey leans slightly closer to the book.
Joey reads, "April 17."
Joey continues reading, "Excessive noise. Italian guy comes home with a date." He smiles and chuckles.
Chandler looks down at the book.
Joey says, "Chandler, look, you're in here too." Chandler reads, "April 18."
Chandler continues reading, "Excessive noise Italian guy's, gay roommate brings home dry cleaning." Joey smiles.
Monica turns and walks away, the corners of her mouth turned up slightly. Joey and Ross look at Chandler.
Chandler stares at the book, his facial expression remaining unchanged and his shoulders still.
Chandler says, "Well, that's excellent," his voice flat and without altering his facial expression.
Rachel approaches Monica carrying a large, shell-covered lamp with a driftwood base.
Rachel says, "Monica."
Rachel gestures with the lamp and says, "Monica, look at this lamp. Is this tacky or what? We have to have this."
Monica looks at the lamp.
Monica says, "Rachel, I think we have enough regular lamps."
Rachel gestures with her free hand and replies, "What? Oh, come on. I mean, it's not like I'm asking for this girly clock or anything," picking up a small clock featuring a blonde doll.
Rachel adds, "Which, by the way, I also think is really cool," gesturing with the doll clock.
Monica shakes her head slightly.
Monica says, "It doesn't go with any of my stuff."
Rachel replies, "Well, what about my stuff? You don't have any stuff. This will be the start of my stuff."
Monica stares at Rachel.
Monica says, "No."
Rachel slightly lowers the lamp.
Rachel asks, "You still think of it as your apartment, don't you?"
Monica shakes her head slightly.
Monica says, "No." Rachel maintains her gaze on Monica.
Rachel says, "Yes, you do."
Rachel continues, "You think of it as your apartment and I'm just someone who rents a room."
Monica averts her gaze toward the floor.
Rachel says, "Okay, while you chew on that for a while I'm going to go find a place for my new lamp." She turns and walks away with the lamp.
Monica watches Rachel leave, then walks in the opposite direction.
Ross approaches Phoebe, holding two small troll dolls. He says, "Okay, Phoebes."
Ross says, "See how I'm making these little toys move?" moving the dolls' arms with his thumbs.
Phoebe watches the dolls and smiles, her teeth showing.
Ross says, "Opposable thumbs."
Phoebe looks up from the dolls to Ross's face.
Ross gestures with the dolls and says, "Without evolution, how do you explain opposable thumbs?"
Phoebe turns her head slightly, her eyes moving away from Ross.
Phoebe says, "Maybe the overlords needed them to steer their spacecraft."
Ross closes his eyes and tilts his head back slightly.
Ross opens his eyes, looks at Phoebe, and says, "Please tell me you're joking."
Phoebe says, "Look, can't we just say that you believe in something and"
Ross interrupts, "no, Phoebes, we can't, okay? Because it's like"
Ross continues, "math. One plus one equals two."
Ross adds, "I can't stand by and let you think that one plus one might equal three or"
Ross finishes, "four or yellow," raising his hands briefly.
Phoebe stares at Ross, her head tilted.
Phoebe asks, "Why not?"
Phoebe asks, "Now, what's that all about?"
Phoebe gestures toward Ross and asks, "What is this obsessive need you have to make everybody agree with you?"
Phoebe continues, "You know what? I think maybe it's time you put"
Phoebe finishes, "Ross under the microscope," pointing at him.
Ross stares at Phoebe with his mouth slightly open, blinking once.
In a cluttered apartment filled with boxes and miscellaneous items, Chandler sits on a couch, holding an open book on his lap. He tilts his head back, looking toward the ceiling.
Chandler says, "Is there blood coming out of my ears?" while maintaining his upward gaze.
Joey walks behind the couch holding a dark book. To the right, Phoebe bends over a box.
"Check it out. Check it out. Heckles high school yearbook," Joey says, standing behind the couch and showing the open book to Chandler. Phoebe turns her head toward them.
Phoebe steps closer and looks down at the book in Joey's hands.
Looking at the book, Phoebe says, "Wow. He looks so normal."
Looking at the pages, Phoebe reads, "He's even kind of cute. Heckles, you crack me up in science class."
Phoebe continues reading the yearbook page.
Phoebe reads further, "You're the funniest kid in school."
Chandler looks toward Joey with a puzzled expression.
"Funniest? Heckles?" Chandler asks. Joey responds, "That's what it says," pointing at the page.
Chandler takes the book from Joey. Reading it, he says, "Wow. Heckles was voted class clown. And so was I."
Chandler looks up from the book. A rhythmic thumping sound begins from the ceiling.
Chandler looks upward and says, "He was right?" as the thumping continues.
The thumping persists. "Would you listen to that? I'd call that excessive," Chandler remarks, keeping his gaze aimed upward.
Chandler abruptly stands up, looking down at the book. "Whoa, what?" he says.
Chandler walks away from the couch, reading aloud from the book, "Heckles played clarinet in band. And I played clarinet."
Chandler stops walking and gestures with one hand while continuing to read, "And he was in the scale modelers club. And I was well, there was no"
Chandler finishes his sentence, "club. But I sure thought they were cool."
Joey stands with his arms crossed, watching Chandler.
"So you were both dorks," Joey states, standing with his arms crossed.
Joey shrugs slightly, maintaining his crossed arms.
"Big deal," Joey adds.
Chandler rests his hand against a stack of newspapers. "I just think it's weird, you know?" he says.
Chandler gestures upward with one hand. "Heckles and me."
Chandler gestures toward his own chest.
"Heckles and me," Chandler repeats, pointing upward and then back to himself.
Chandler turns his head and gazes back up at the ceiling.
"and Heckles..." Chandler says, walking to the door. He grabs a red broom, thrusts the handle up against the ceiling, and yells, "would you knock it off?"
The broom handle strikes the ceiling, and the brush end swings down and hits Chandler on the head.
Chandler drops the broom, raises his hands defensively near his head, and yells, "I'm."
Chandler stands frozen with his hands raised near his face, his mouth open, and his eyes wide.
The scene changes to a sunrise view of the New York City skyline with a large bridge structure partially visible in the foreground.
Now inside a dimly lit apartment, Chandler sits on a couch wearing a white t-shirt, plaid pajama pants, and a dark plaid robe. A small, open green box rests on his lap.
Chandler stares forward. Joey walks into the room carrying a black garbage bag, and Chandler turns his head to look at him.
Joey asks, "Have you been here all night?" Chandler pulls a small photograph from the box.
Chandler says, "Look at this. Pictures of all the women that Heckles went out with. Look what he wrote"
Chandler continues, "on them. Vivian too tall," showing the photo to Joey, who sits down beside him.
Chandler tosses the photo and picks up another one, reading, "Madge big."
Chandler finishes reading the note on the photo, "Gums."
Chandler picks up another photograph from the box.
"Too loud," Chandler reads.
Chandler picks up a small card, reading from it, "Too smart. Makes noise when she eats."
Chandler points to his chest repeatedly and says, "This is me. This is what I do."
Chandler points at himself repeatedly, then gestures downward toward the items in the box.
"I'm going to end up alone. Just like he did," Chandler states, his eyes wide as he looks straight ahead.
Joey replies, "Chandler, Heckles was a nutcase." Chandler stands up, gesturing emphatically, and says, "Our trains are on the same track. Okay, yeah,"
Chandler points at his chest, his volume rising. "sure. I'm coming up 30 years behind them. But the stops are all the same. Bittertown," he says, pointing his thumb over his shoulder.
Chandler gestures forward with one hand, his arm extended toward Joey.
"Aloneville. Hermit Junction," Chandler lists, extending both hands outward.
Chandler stands with his arms out to his sides, his brow furrowed as he looks at Joey.
Chandler maintains his wide stance and open-armed gesture as he continues to stare at Joey. The clip ends.
